content.summary: Learn how to send and receive NDI (Network Device Interface) video streams in TouchDesigner. NDI allows low-latency video transmission over a local network between applications and machines, making it ideal for multi-machine setups, live production, and integration with other NDI-compatible software.

sections.title: Understanding NDI
sections.content:
NDI (Network Device Interface) is a royalty-free protocol for transmitting video, audio, and metadata over standard Ethernet networks. TouchDesigner has built-in NDI support through the **NDI In TOP** and **NDI Out TOP** operators.

Key advantages:
- **Low latency**: Typically under one frame of delay on a local network
- **Auto-discovery**: NDI sources are automatically discoverable on the network
- **No special hardware**: Works over standard gigabit Ethernet
- **Cross-application**: Send to/from OBS, Resolume, Unity, and many other tools

sections.title: Sending NDI Output
sections.content:
To send a video stream from TouchDesigner, create an **NDI Out TOP** and connect any TOP to its input. The stream immediately becomes available on the network.

The NDI Out TOP's `Source Name` parameter sets the name that other applications will see when browsing for NDI sources. By default it uses the machine name and operator path.
sections.code:
# Create an NDI output
ndi_out = op('/project1').create(ndioutTOP, 'ndi_out')
ndi_out.par.sourcename = 'TD_MainOutput'

# Connect your content to it
# (wire any TOP chain into ndi_out's input)

sections.title: Receiving NDI Input
sections.content:
Create an **NDI In TOP** to receive video from any NDI source on your network. Click the `Source Name` parameter's dropdown menu to see all available NDI sources — they are auto-discovered via mDNS.

The NDI In TOP outputs the received video as a standard TOP texture that you can process, composite, or render like any other image in TouchDesigner.
sections.code:
# Create an NDI input
ndi_in = op('/project1').create(ndiinTOP, 'ndi_in')
# List available NDI sources
print(ndi_in.par.sourcename.menuNames)
# Select a source by name
ndi_in.par.sourcename = 'MACHINE_NAME (TD_MainOutput)'

sections.title: Performance Considerations
sections.content:
NDI works best on **gigabit Ethernet** or faster. For optimal performance:

- Use a dedicated network for NDI traffic when possible
- Lower resolution reduces bandwidth: 1080p NDI uses roughly 125 Mbps
- The NDI Out TOP's `Bandwidth` parameter lets you choose between full bandwidth and a compressed mode that reduces network load at the cost of some quality
- Monitor network utilization in the **Performance Monitor** (F2)
- Multiple NDI streams on one machine are supported but increase CPU/GPU load

sections.title: Multi-Machine Workflow
sections.content:
A common production setup uses multiple TouchDesigner machines connected via NDI:

1. **Content machines** generate visuals and send via NDI Out
2. **A mixing machine** receives all streams via NDI In and composites them
3. **An output machine** receives the final mix and sends to displays

All machines must be on the same subnet for auto-discovery. If machines are on different subnets, use the NDI Access Manager to manually add IP addresses.
sections.code:
# Example: receive two NDI streams and composite them
ndi_a = op('/project1/ndi_in_a')
ndi_b = op('/project1/ndi_in_b')

# Create a Composite TOP to blend them
comp = op('/project1').create(compositeTOP, 'ndi_mix')
comp.par.operand = 'over'
# Wire ndi_a and ndi_b into comp's inputs
